sections.heading: Definitions
sections.title: Key banking definitions for financial regulation
sections.summary: This section provides detailed definitions for critical banking terms, including 'bank', 'banking group', 'additional tier 1 capital', 'common equity tier 1 capital', and 'deposit'. It specifies the regulatory framework for financial institutions and clarifies terms used in the Banks Act and Financial Sector Regulation Act.

requirements.text: A bank is a public company registered as a bank in terms of this Act
A banking group consists of two or more persons predominantly engaged in financial activities, with one being a bank
Additional tier 1 capital refers to shares subordinated to depositors but above common equity tier 1 capital
Common equity tier 1 capital represents the most subordinated claim in liquidation
A deposit is an amount of money paid subject to repayment conditions and interest terms

sections.source_quote: 1. Definitions (1) In this Act, unless the context otherwise indicates-

sections.heading: Conditions of registration
sections.title: Bank registration conditions and foreign branches
sections.summary: Bank registration is subject to conditions set by the Authority. Foreign institutions may establish branches in South Africa with prior authorization, provided they meet supervisory requirements and risk management standards. The Authority may cancel or suspend authorization for non-compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes conditions for bank registration and foreign branch operations, impacting cross-border banking by requiring foreign institutions to obtain authorization and meet regulatory standards before operating in South Africa.

requirements.text: Bank registration is subject to conditions set by Authority
Foreign institutions may establish branches with Authority authorization
Foreign institutions must submit application with prescribed information
Foreign institutions must pay prescribed fee
Authority may require additional information on supervisory practices
Authority must ensure foreign institutions have proper supervision
Authority may cancel or suspend authorization for non-compliance
Authority must provide written notice of cancellation/suspension intent
Authority must specify reasons for cancellation/suspension
Authority must allow 30-day period for response to cancellation/suspension

sections.source_quote: 18. Bank registration conditions and foreign branches (1) An institution which has been established in a country other than the Republic and which lawfully conducts in such other country a business similar to the business of a bank (hereinafter in this section referred to as the foreign institution) may, notwithstanding the provisions of section 11(1), with the prior written authorization of the Authority and subject to the prescribed conditions and to such further conditions, if any, as the Authority may determine, conduct the business of a bank by means of a branch in the Republic.

sections.heading: Publication of information relating to banks, controlling companies, eligible institutions and representative offices of foreign
sections.title: Public publication and record-keeping of bank information
sections.summary: The Authority must publish in the Gazette and maintain records of bank registrations, cancellations, suspensions, restrictions, name changes, mergers, asset transfers exceeding 25%, and approvals for subsidiaries, branches, and foreign operations. This includes detailed records of all approvals granted under the Act for international banking activities.

requirements.text: The Authority shall publish a notice in the Gazette and shall keep a record of registration of an institution as a bank or a controlling company.
The Authority shall keep a record of cancellation or suspension of the registration of a bank or controlling company.
The Authority shall keep a record of withdrawal of the authorisation to conduct the business of a bank by means of a branch.
The Authority shall keep a record of restriction on the activities of a bank, controlling company or branch.
The Authority shall keep a record of change of name of a bank, controlling company, branch or representative office of a foreign institution.

The Authority shall keep a record of permission granted in respect of the amalgamation, merger or arrangement referred to in Chapter 5 of the Companies Act, where a bank is the principal party to such amalgamation, merger or arrangement.
The Authority shall keep a record of permission granted to an arrangement for the transfer of more than 25 per cent of the assets, liabilities or assets and liabilities of a bank to another person.
The Authority shall keep a record of approval granted in respect of an eligible institution, which is effected or which takes place under this Act.
The Authority shall keep a record of every approval granted to a bank or a controlling company to establish or acquire a subsidiary within or outside the Republic.
The Authority shall keep a record of every approval granted to a bank to establish or acquire a branch of a bank.

The Authority shall keep a record of every approval granted to a bank or a controlling company to acquire an interest in any undertaking having its registered office or principal place of business outside the Republic.
The Authority shall keep a record of every approval granted to a bank or a controlling company to create or acquire a trust outside the Republic of which the bank is a major beneficiary.

The Authority shall keep a record of every approval granted to a bank or a controlling company to establish or acquire any financial or other business undertaking under its direct or indirect control.
The Authority shall keep a record of every approval granted to a bank to establish or acquire a representative office outside the Republic.

sections.source_quote: 30. Publication of information relating to banks, controlling companies, eligible institutions and representative offices of foreign institutions and the keeping of records by the Authority (1) The Authority shall publish a notice in the Gazette and shall keep a record of- (a) (i) registration of an institution as a bank or a controlling company; (ii) authorisation granted to conduct the business of a bank by means of a branch; or (iii) consent granted for the establishment of a representative office by a foreign institution; (b) (i) cancellation or suspension of the registration of a bank or controlling company; (ii) withdrawal of the authorisation to conduct the business of a bank by means of a branch; or (iii) withdrawal of consent to conduct the business of a representative office by a foreign institution or the closure of such representative office; (c) restriction on the activities of a bank, controlling company or branch; (d) change of name of a bank, controlling company, branch or representative office of a foreign institution; (e) permission granted in respect of the amalgamation, merger or arrangement referred to in Chapter 5 of the Companies Act, where a bank is the principal party to such amalgamation, merger or arrangement; (f) permission granted to an arrangement for the transfer of more than 25 per cent of the assets, liabilities or assets and liabilities of a bank to another person; or (g) approval granted in respect of an eligible institution, which is effected or which takes place under this Act.

sections.title: Key banking definitions for financial regulation
sections.summary: This section provides detailed definitions for critical banking terms, including 'bank', 'banking group', 'additional tier 1 capital', 'common equity tier 1 capital', and 'deposit'. It specifies the regulatory framework for financial institutions and clarifies terms used in the Banks Act and Financial Sector Regulation Act.

requirements.text: A bank is a public company registered as a bank in terms of this Act
A banking group consists of two or more persons predominantly engaged in financial activities, with one being a bank
Additional tier 1 capital refers to shares subordinated to depositors but above common equity tier 1 capital
