sections.source_quote: 1. Definitions (1) In this Act, unless the context otherwise indicates-

sections.heading: Definitions (cont.)
sections.title: Definitions of financial and banking terms
sections.summary: This section defines key terms used in the Banks Act, including 'deposit-taking institution', 'director', 'division', 'domestic shareholder', 'eligible institution', 'employee in charge of risk management', 'executive officer', 'external credit assessment', 'fellow subsidiary', 'Financial Sector Regulation Act', 'liquid assets', 'level one high-quality liquid assets', 'level two high-quality liquid assets', 'holding company', 'host supervisor', 'hybrid-debt instrument', 'Land Bank', 'prudential standard', 'public company', and others. Each definition specifies the precise legal meaning and references relevant legislation or regulatory frameworks.
sections.dtia_summary: These definitions establish the precise scope of terms critical to data processing and cross-border operations, particularly for financial institutions. They clarify what constitutes a 'bank' or 'deposit-taking institution' and how risks are categorized, which directly impacts data transfer and processing activities.

requirements.text: The term 'deposit-taking institution' refers to a bank or building society conducting business under a name including 'bank' or 'deposit-taking institution'.
The term 'director' includes both executive and non-executive directors as defined in the Companies Act.
A 'division' of a bank is a business unit or section that conducts business under a name including 'bank' or 'deposit-taking institution'.
A 'domestic shareholder' of a bank or controlling company is a shareholder who is resident in the Republic or controlled by a person resident in the Republic.
An 'eligible institution' is an external credit assessment institution or export credit agency approved by the Authority.

An 'employee in charge of a risk management function' is the employee ultimately responsible for managing solvency, liquidity, credit, currency, market, interest rate, counterparty, technological, operational, and compliance risks.
An 'executive officer' includes directors, risk management employees, compliance officers, and secretaries of a bank.
An 'external credit assessment' is an assessment or rating issued by an eligible institution that relates to the ongoing ability of a person or country to repay debts.
A 'fellow subsidiary' means any other company that is a subsidiary of the holding company of which the first-mentioned company is a subsidiary.
The 'Financial Sector Regulation Act' refers to the Financial Sector Regulation Act, 2017.
The term 'liquid assets' has been substituted by 'level one high-quality liquid assets' and 'level two high-quality liquid assets' as defined in the Act.

Level one high-quality liquid assets include cash, gold coin and bullion, central bank reserves, and marketable securities representing claims on sovereigns, central banks, or multilateral development banks.
Level two high-quality liquid assets include marketable securities representing claims on sovereigns, central banks, or multilateral development banks, and corporate bonds.
A 'holding company' has the meaning ascribed to it in the Companies Act.
A 'host supervisor' refers to the supervisor responsible for regulating a foreign branch or subsidiary of a bank within its jurisdiction.
A 'hybrid-debt instrument' is a financial instrument that combines features of equity and debt financial instruments.
The 'Land Bank' means the Land and Agricultural Bank of South Africa.
The term 'prudential standard' has the same meaning as in the Financial Sector Regulation Act.
A 'public company' includes a national state-owned company as envisaged in the Companies Act.

sections.source_quote: Insurance Act, 1998, and under which policy that long-term insurer assumes, in return for such premium, such obligation as is described in the Long-term Insurance Act, 1998; [Subpara. (vi) amended by GN R1765/91 and substituted by s. 1 of Act 20/2007]

sections.heading: Definitions (cont.)
sections.title: Definitions of banking terms and regulatory entities
sections.summary: This section defines key terms used in the Banks Act, including 'qualifying capital and reserve funds', 'Register', 'Reserve Bank', 'subsidiary', and 'the business of a bank'. It specifies the scope of banking activities, excludes certain activities like co-operative borrowing and public sector activities, and outlines the regulatory framework for financial institutions. The definitions are critical for determining what constitutes a bank and its operational boundaries under the Act. This section defines critical terms used in the Banks Act, including 'unimpaired reserve funds', 'Tribunal', 'undesirable practice', and 'wholly owned subsidiary'. These definitions establish the legal framework for assessing the fitness of directors and officers, ensuring clarity in regulatory compliance for banking entities.
sections.dtia_summary: These definitions establish the legal framework for what constitutes a bank and its activities, which is essential for determining data transfer applicability under cross-border contexts. The precise definition of 'the business of a bank' helps identify which data transfers require specific safeguards. These definitions are foundational for DTIAs as they specify terms that may impact cross-border data transfers, particularly regarding the regulatory framework for banking operations and the Financial Services Tribunal.

requirements.text: qualifying capital and reserve funds means the net sum of capital and reserve funds required to be held by a bank, calculated and determined in accordance with the provisions of section 70(2), 70(2A) or 70(2B), as the case may be, having regard to the nature of such bank's business
Register means the Financial Sector Information Register referred to in section 256 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
Regulation means a regulation made under section 90
Regulations relating to Banks means the Regulations relating to Banks as amended or re-enacted from time to time under section 90

Regulations relating to branches means the Regulations titled 'Conditions for the conducting of the business of a bank by a foreign institution by means of a branch in the Republic' published by Government Notice of 1 January 2008, and amended from time to time

representative office, in relation to a foreign institution referred to in section 34(1), means premises situated within the Republic and from which the business referred to in section 34(1) is promoted or assisted in any way
representative office, in relation to a bank, means premises situated outside the Republic and from which the business conducted by such bank within the Republic is promoted or assisted in any way
Republic, for the purposes of the definition of 'domestic shareholder', includes any state the territory of which formerly formed part of the Republic
Reserve Bank means the South African Reserve Bank
subsidiary means a subsidiary provided for in section 3 of the Companies Act
the business of a bank means the acceptance of deposits from the general public as a regular feature of the business
the business of a bank means the soliciting of or advertising for deposits

the business of a bank means the utilization of money, or of the interest or other income earned on money, accepted by way of deposit, for the granting of loans, investment, or financing of other business activities
the business of a bank means the obtaining of money through the sale of an asset to any person other than a bank, subject to an agreement for the seller to purchase the asset at a future date
the business of a bank means any other activity declared by the Authority after consultation with the Governor of the Reserve Bank
the business of a bank does not include the acceptance of deposits by a person who does not purport to accept deposits on a regular basis and has not advertised for or solicited such deposits
the business of a bank does not include the borrowing of money from its members by a co-operative subject to conditions as prescribed
the business of a bank does not include any activity of a public sector, governmental or other institution, or of any person designated by the Authority with the approval of the Minister
the business of a bank does not include activities performed by institutions registered under other Acts of Parliament or schemes authorized by other Acts of Parliament
tier 2 capital means instruments or shares subordinated to depositors and general creditors that do not constitute common equity tier 1 or additional tier 1 capital
tier 2 unimpaired reserve funds means any premium arising from the issue of instruments or shares that constitute tier 2 capital
Tribunal means the Financial Services Tribunal established in terms of section 219 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
undesirable practice means any act prohibited, or any failure to perform any act enjoined, by section 78(1)
wholly owned subsidiary means a wholly owned subsidiary as defined in section 1 of the Companies Act

sections.source_quote: qualifying capital and reserve funds means the net sum of capital and reserve funds required to be held by a bank, calculated and determined in accordance with the provisions of section 70(2), 70(2A) or 70(2B), as the case may be, having regard to the nature of such bank's business

sections.heading: Definitions (cont.)
sections.title: Qualifications for banking officers and regulatory oversight
sections.summary: This section defines the qualifications and conduct requirements for individuals serving in banking roles, including the need for competence, sound judgment, diligence, and adherence to legal standards. It specifies that the Authority may consider previous convictions, misconduct, or involvement in fraudulent activities when assessing an individual's suitability for banking roles.
sections.dtia_summary: These definitions establish the criteria for assessing the competence of individuals handling financial data, which is critical for data processing and transfer activities involving sensitive financial information.

requirements.text: The Authority may consider the competence and soundness of judgment of a person for the fulfillment of banking responsibilities.
The Authority may consider the diligence with which a person fulfills banking responsibilities.
The Authority may consider previous convictions for fraud or other offenses involving dishonesty or violence.
The Authority may consider contraventions of laws designed to protect the public from financial loss due to dishonesty or incompetence.
The Authority may consider involvement in business practices that are deceitful, prejudicial, or otherwise improper.
The Authority may consider any other business practices that cast doubt on a person's competence or soundness of judgment.
The Authority may request a person to complete a questionnaire to assess their suitability for banking roles.
A person who refuses to comply with the Authority's request for a questionnaire is guilty of an offense.

sections.source_quote: The Authority may have regard to the previous conduct and activities of the person concerned in business or financial matters and, in particular, to any evidence that such person was convicted of the offence of fraud or any other offence of which dishonesty, or the commission of violence, was an element;

sections.heading: Exclusions from application of Act
sections.title: Exclusions from application of the Act
sections.summary: This section specifies that the Banks Act does not apply to certain institutions, including the Reserve Bank, Land Bank, Development Bank of Southern Africa, Corporation for Public Deposits, Public Investment Corporation Limited, mutual banks, co-operative banks registered under the Co-operative Banks Act, and any other institution designated by the Minister.
sections.dtia_summary: These exclusions define which institutions are exempt from the Banks Act, which is critical for determining the scope of data processing and transfer obligations for financial entities.

requirements.text: The Banks Act does not apply to the Reserve Bank, Land Bank, Development Bank of Southern Africa, Corporation for Public Deposits, Public Investment Corporation Limited, mutual banks, co-operative banks registered under the Co-operative Banks Act, and any other institution designated by the Minister.

sections.source_quote: Except where expressly stated otherwise, the provisions of this Act, in so far as they impose requirements with which any institution must comply before it may carry on the business of a bank or in the lawful carrying on of the business of a bank, shall not apply to the Reserve Bank; the Land Bank; the Development Bank of Southern Africa; the Corporation for Public Deposits established by section 2 of the Corporation for Public Deposits Act,  1984; the Public Investment Corporation Limited established by section 2 of the Public Investment Corporation Act, 2004; any mutual bank; a co-operative bank registered under the Co-operative Banks Act, 2007; or any other institution or body designated by the Minister by notice in the Gazette.

sections.heading: Authority
sections.title: Authority's cooperation arrangements with foreign supervisors
sections.summary: Section 4 authorizes the Authority to enter into written cooperation arrangements with host supervisors or other institutions. These arrangements can include accepting foreign institutions' approval processes, conducting on-site examinations, sharing financial information, and receiving adverse assessments of foreign operations. The Authority may also share information about significant problems within banks. These provisions apply to the Authority and foreign banks operating in South Africa.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes mechanisms for international cooperation between South African banking regulators and foreign supervisors, which impacts cross-border data transfers by enabling information sharing and joint examinations of foreign banking entities.

requirements.text: The Authority may enter into written cooperation arrangements with host supervisors or institutions
The Authority may accept foreign institutions' approval processes if they comply with prescribed conditions
The Authority may conduct on-site examinations of foreign banks within host jurisdictions
Host supervisors may conduct on-site examinations of foreign bank entities
The Authority may share financial condition information with host supervisors
The Authority must be informed of adverse assessments of foreign operations
The Authority may provide information about significant problems in banks

sections.source_quote: The Authority may from time to time enter into a written cooperation arrangement, including a memorandum of understanding, with a host supervisor, consolidating supervisor or any other person or institution as the Authority may deem fit

sections.heading: Delegation of powers and assignment of functions by Authority
sections.title: Delegation of regulatory powers to bank officials
sections.summary: Section 5 allows the Authority to delegate powers to Reserve Bank officers or other financial regulators with Reserve Bank approval. Delegated powers include performing duties under the Act. This delegation does not prevent the Authority from exercising the delegated powers themselves. The section applies to the Authority and Reserve Bank officers.
sections.dtia_summary: This section enables regulatory powers to be delegated to bank officials, which may affect how data transfers are supervised across borders through delegated authority mechanisms.

requirements.text: The Authority may delegate powers to Reserve Bank officers with Reserve Bank approval
The Authority may authorize officers to perform duties under this Act
Delegated powers do not prevent the Authority from exercising them

sections.source_quote: The Authority may with the approval of the Reserve Bank delegate to any officer or employee of the Reserve Bank or another financial sector regulator any power conferred upon the Authority by or under this Act

sections.heading: Powers of inspection of, and guide-lines by, Authority
sections.title: Authority's inspection powers and guidance mechanisms
sections.summary: Section 6 grants the Authority powers to conduct inspections and issue guidelines to banks and financial institutions. It allows for discussions with bank executives, issuance of directives with sanctions, and guidance on market practices. The Authority may issue directives requiring banks to take specific actions within specified timeframes. This section applies to the Authority and banks.
sections.dtia_summary: This section provides the Authority with inspection powers and directive mechanisms that impact cross-border data transfers through regulatory oversight of foreign banking entities.

requirements.text: The Authority may issue written directives to banks after consultation
Directives may require banks to cease or refrain from certain activities
Directives may require banks to perform actions to comply with directives
Directives may require banks to provide information to the Authority
The Authority may cancel previously issued directives after consultation
Directives have no retroactive effect
Banks that neglect directives are guilty of an offence

sections.source_quote: The Authority may from time to time, in writing, after consultation with the relevant bank, ... issue a directive to such a bank ... regarding the application of the Act

sections.heading: Furnishing of information by banks and controlling companies
sections.title: Information disclosure requirements for banks
sections.summary: Section 7 requires banks to provide information to the Authority when directed. The Authority may direct banks to furnish specific information or reports by public accountants. Banks must provide audit reports to the Authority and cannot disclose these reports to third parties without written consent. This section applies to banks and the Authority.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes information disclosure requirements that impact data transfers by mandating banks to share financial information with regulators, affecting cross-border data flows.

requirements.text: The Authority may direct banks to furnish specific information
The Authority may direct banks to provide audit reports by public accountants
Banks must pay for audit reports
The Authority may require reports to be in specified formats
Banks cannot disclose audit reports to third parties without Authority consent

sections.source_quote: The Authority may by notice in writing direct a bank or a controlling company ... to furnish the Authority with such information as may be specified in the notice

sections.heading: Registration a prerequisite for conducting business of bank
sections.title: Bank registration requirement
sections.summary: Section 11 states that no person may conduct bank business unless they are a public company registered as a bank under this Act. This provision applies to all bank applicants and is a prerequisite for banking operations in South Africa.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes the requirement for bank registration, which impacts data transfers by ensuring only registered entities can handle financial data across borders.

requirements.text: No person may conduct bank business without being a public company registered as a bank

sections.source_quote: Subject to the provisions of section 18A, no person shall conduct the business of a bank unless such person is a public company and is registered as a bank in terms of this Act

sections.heading: Application for authorization to establish bank
sections.title: Bank authorization application process
sections.summary: Section 12 outlines the process for applying to establish a bank, including submitting applications in prescribed forms and providing financial information. State-owned companies must meet specific asset requirements before applying for bank authorization. This section applies to bank applicants and the Authority.
sections.dtia_summary: This section details the authorization process for banks, which impacts data transfers by requiring applicants to meet financial and regulatory criteria before handling cross-border data.

requirements.text: Applicants must submit applications in prescribed forms
Applicants must provide financial information as required by the Authority
State-owned companies must have assets exceeding liabilities for 24 months

sections.source_quote: Any person who wishes to conduct the business of a bank may apply to the Authority for authorization to establish such a bank

sections.heading: Granting or refusal of application for authorization
sections.title: Authorization application approval conditions
sections.summary: The Authority may grant or refuse bank establishment applications based on submitted information, subject to conditions. The Authority must verify the applicant's public interest alignment, bank business scope, public company status under Companies Act, successful establishment capability, financial resources, prudent banking practices, and fit/proper directors. Foreign applicants require additional supervisory compliance and risk management validation.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes conditions for bank authorization that impact cross-border operations by requiring foreign institutions to demonstrate regulatory compliance and risk management standards before establishing branches in South Africa.

requirements.text: Authority may grant or refuse bank establishment applications after considering submitted information
Authority must verify applicant's public interest alignment
Authority must confirm applicant conducts bank business
Authority must verify applicant is a public company registered under Companies Act
Authority must ensure applicant can establish bank successfully
Authority must confirm applicant has financial means to comply with Act
Authority must verify applicant conducts business prudently
Authority must confirm all directors are fit and proper persons
Authority must verify directors have sufficient business management experience
Authority must ensure board composition is appropriate for business scale
Foreign applicants must have authorized bank establishment in Republic
Foreign applicants must comply with Basel Committee on Banking Supervision
Foreign applicants must not be legally impeded from complying with Basel Committee
Foreign applicants must accept responsibilities as consolidating supervisors
Foreign applicants must ensure board and management consist of fit and proper persons
Foreign applicants must verify risk management standards
Foreign applicants must keep Authority informed of financial soundness

sections.source_quote: 13. Granting or refusal of application for authorization (1) Subject to the provisions of subsection (2), the Authority may, after considering all information, documents and reports furnished to him or her for the purposes of an application under section 12, grant or refuse the relevant application or grant the application subject to such conditions as he or she may determine.

sections.heading: Revocation of authorization
sections.title: Revocation of bank authorization
sections.summary: The Authority may revoke bank authorization if false information was provided in the application or if the bank fails to form within 12 months of authorization. Revocation requires written notice to the applicant. This section addresses the consequences of non-compliance with authorization conditions.
sections.dtia_summary: This section allows revocation of bank authorization for non-compliance, impacting cross-border operations by requiring foreign institutions to maintain authorization validity through timely formation and compliance.

requirements.text: Authority may revoke authorization if false or misleading information was furnished
Authority may revoke authorization if bank fails to form within 12 months
Authority must provide written notice of revocation to applicant

sections.source_quote: 14. Revocation of authorization (1) The Authority may at any time prior to the registration, in terms of section 17, of a bank, revoke the authorization granted for the establishment of such a bank if the Authority is satisfied that- (a) false or misleading information was furnished in the application for such authorization; or (b) success has not been achieved within a period of 12 months as from the date of the granting of the said authorization, with the formation, in accordance with the proposals contained in the application for the said authorization, of the proposed bank.

sections.heading: Formation of certain companies prohibited except with approval of Authority
sections.title: Bank formation approval requirements
sections.summary: No bank can be formed without the Authority's written approval. The Authority grants approval only if the company is likely to be eligible for registration as a bank under this Act. This section ensures that only compliant entities can establish banking operations.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires bank formation to be approved by the Authority, impacting cross-border operations by ensuring foreign institutions must obtain authorization before establishing banking entities in South Africa.

requirements.text: No public company may form bank without Authority's written approval
Authority grants approval only if company is likely to be eligible for bank registration

sections.source_quote: 15. Formation of certain companies prohibited except with approval of Authority (1) No public company shall without the written approval of the Authority be formed in terms of the Companies Act to conduct the business of a bank in accordance with the provisions of this Act.

sections.heading: Application for registration as bank
sections.title: Bank registration application process
sections.summary: Applicants with authorization may apply for bank registration within 12 months. Applications require specific documents including memorandum of incorporation, business details, director information, and shareholder lists. The Authority may request additional information to assess compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: This section outlines the application process for bank registration, impacting cross-border operations by requiring detailed documentation from foreign institutions seeking to establish banking operations in South Africa.

requirements.text: Applicant may apply for registration within 12 months of authorization
Application must be made in prescribed form and manner
Application must include two copies of memorandum of incorporation
Application must include business details and business conduct plan
Application must include full names and addresses of directors and executives
Application must include shareholder list as of application date
Authority may require additional information for assessment

sections.source_quote: 16. Application for registration as bank (1) An applicant to whom the Authority has under section 13 granted authorization for the establishment of a bank (hereinafter in this Chapter referred to as the institution) may at any time during the period of 12 months commencing on the date of the granting of the said authorization apply to the Authority for the registration of the institution as a bank, provided such authorization has not been revoked in terms of section 14(1).

sections.heading: Granting or refusal of application for registration
sections.title: Bank registration approval conditions
sections.summary: The Authority grants bank registration if the applicant conducts bank business, avoids undesirable practices, and has a compliant memorandum of incorporation. The Authority may refuse registration if the institution fails to meet conditions, poses compliance risks, or has naming conflicts with existing banks.
sections.dtia_summary: This section sets conditions for bank registration that impact cross-border operations by requiring foreign institutions to demonstrate compliance with local banking standards before operating in South Africa.

requirements.text: Authority grants registration if applicant conducts bank business
Authority grants registration if applicant avoids undesirable business practices
Authority grants registration if memorandum of incorporation is compliant
Authority may refuse registration if institution fails to meet section 13 conditions
Authority may refuse registration if institution likely to violate Act provisions
Authority may refuse registration if institutional interests conflict with Act
Authority may refuse registration if depositors' interests are detrimentally affected
Authority may refuse registration if name conflicts with existing banks

sections.source_quote: 17. Granting or refusal of application for registration (1) Subject to the provisions of subsection (2), the Authority shall, after considering all information and documents furnished to him or her in terms of section 16 for the purposes of an application under that section, grant such application if he or she is satisfied- (a) that the business the applicant proposes to conduct is that of a bank; (b) that the applicant does not propose to adopt undesirable methods of conducting business; and (c) that the memorandum of incorporation of the institution is consistent with this Act and is not undesirable for any reason.

sections.heading: Conditions of registration
sections.title: Bank registration conditions and foreign branches
sections.summary: Bank registration is subject to conditions set by the Authority. Foreign institutions may establish branches in South Africa with prior authorization, provided they meet supervisory requirements and risk management standards. The Authority may cancel or suspend authorization for non-compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes conditions for bank registration and foreign branch operations, impacting cross-border banking by requiring foreign institutions to obtain authorization and meet regulatory standards before operating in South Africa.

requirements.text: Bank registration is subject to conditions set by Authority
Foreign institutions may establish branches with Authority authorization
Foreign institutions must submit application with prescribed information
Foreign institutions must pay prescribed fee
Authority may require additional information on supervisory practices
Authority must ensure foreign institutions have proper supervision
Authority may cancel or suspend authorization for non-compliance
Authority must provide written notice of cancellation/suspension intent
Authority must specify reasons for cancellation/suspension
Authority must allow 30-day period for response to cancellation/suspension

sections.source_quote: 18. Bank registration conditions and foreign branches (1) An institution which has been established in a country other than the Republic and which lawfully conducts in such other country a business similar to the business of a bank (hereinafter in this section referred to as the foreign institution) may, notwithstanding the provisions of section 11(1), with the prior written authorization of the Authority and subject to the prescribed conditions and to such further conditions, if any, as the Authority may determine, conduct the business of a bank by means of a branch in the Republic.

sections.heading: Untrue information in connection with applications
sections.title: Prohibition on providing false information in bank applications
sections.summary: Any person who provides untrue or misleading information to the Authority when applying for bank authorization or registration is guilty of an offence. This section imposes a direct obligation on applicants to ensure all information submitted is truthful and accurate. The Authority is the relevant regulatory body responsible for enforcing this provision.
sections.dtia_summary: This section does not directly impact data transfers but requires applicants to provide accurate information, which indirectly affects data quality and compliance in financial applications.

requirements.text: Any person who furnishes the Authority with any information which to the knowledge of such person is untrue or misleading in any material respect shall be guilty of an offence

sections.source_quote: Any person who in or in connection with- (a) an application for authorization to establish a bank; or (b) an application for registration as a bank, furnishes the Authority with any information which to the knowledge of such person is untrue or misleading in any material respect, shall be guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: Use of name of bank
sections.title: Prohibition on using unauthorized bank names
sections.summary: Banks and foreign institutions authorized to conduct banking business in South Africa must use their registered names or approved translations/abbreviations. They may use alternative names only with Authority consent, such as names of amalgamated banks or divisions. The Authority may permit names otherwise generally known. This section prohibits using names that imply banking status without authorization.
sections.dtia_summary: This section affects how banks identify themselves, which is relevant for data transfer contexts where naming conventions impact data handling and third-party relationships.

requirements.text: An institution registered as a bank or foreign institution authorized to conduct banking business in the Republic must not use a name other than its registered name
An institution may use an alternative name with Authority consent, such as a name of a bank it has amalgamated with
A bank may use a division, brand, or product name with Authority consent, provided it includes the word 'bank' or derivative
No person may use names indicating they are a bank without being registered as such
A company may use names including 'bank' or 'deposit-taking institution' only if it is registered as a bank or authorized under this Act

sections.source_quote: Any person who, in connection with any business conducted by such person- (a) uses any name, description or symbol indicating, or calculated to lead persons to infer, that such person is a bank registered as such under this Act; or (b) in any other manner purports to be a bank registered as such under this Act, while such person is not so registered as a bank, shall be guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: Cancellation or suspension of registration by Authority
sections.title: Cancellation of bank registration for non-compliance
sections.summary: The Authority may cancel or suspend a bank's registration if it has not conducted business for six months, has been convicted of an offence under section 21, has lost authorization in another country, or has failed to comply with conditions. This section outlines specific grounds for terminating a bank's operational status.
sections.dtia_summary: This section impacts the operational status of banks, which affects data transfer capabilities and compliance with regulatory requirements for financial institutions.

requirements.text: The Authority may cancel or suspend registration if the bank has not conducted business for six months
The Authority may cancel or suspend registration if the bank has been convicted of an offence under section 21
The Authority may cancel or suspend registration if the bank's foreign authorization is revoked
The Authority may cancel or suspend registration if the bank fails to comply with conditions

sections.source_quote: The Authority may subject to the provisions of section 24, in the case of a bank registered as such, by notice in writing to the institution concerned cancel, or suspend on such conditions as the Authority may deem fit, such registration if the institution has not conducted any business as a bank during the period of six months commencing on the date on which the institution was registered as a bank.

sections.heading: Notice by Authority of intention to cancel or suspend registration
sections.title: Notice requirements for registration cancellation
sections.summary: The Authority must provide written notice to the bank before cancelling or suspending registration, including reasons for cancellation and a 30-day period for the bank to respond. This section ensures procedural fairness and transparency in regulatory actions.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes procedural requirements for regulatory actions that impact banks, which is relevant for data transfer contexts where regulatory status affects data handling.

requirements.text: The Authority must inform the institution of its intention to cancel registration
The Authority must furnish the institution with reasons for intended cancellation
The Authority must allow the institution 30 days to show cause why registration should not be cancelled

sections.source_quote: The Authority may by written notice to the chairperson or chief executive officer of the institution concerned suspend the registration of a bank under section 23 with immediate effect, provided that the Authority must- (a) inform the institution of his or her intention to cancel such registration; (b) furnish the institution with the reasons for the intended cancellation; and (c) call upon the institution to show cause within a period specified in the notice, which shall not be less than 30 days as from the date of the notice, why its registration should not be so cancelled.

sections.heading: Cancellation or suspension of registration by court
sections.title: Court-ordered cancellation of bank registration
sections.summary: The Authority may apply to a court for cancellation or suspension of a bank's registration if there are grounds beyond those in section 23. The court considers factors such as criminal convictions, business conduct, compliance with regulations, and public interest. This section provides a legal mechanism for resolving disputes over bank registration.
sections.dtia_summary: This section allows for court intervention in bank registration cancellations, which can impact data transfer capabilities and regulatory compliance for financial institutions.

requirements.text: The Authority may apply to a court for cancellation or suspension of registration if there are grounds other than those in section 23
The court may cancel or suspend registration if the bank has been convicted of an offence under this Act
The court may cancel or suspend registration if the bank does not carry on satisfactory banking business
The court may cancel or suspend registration if the bank fails to comply with applicable requirements
The court may cancel or suspend registration if the bank continues to employ undesirable practices
The court may cancel or suspend registration if the bank materially misrepresents its facilities

sections.source_quote: The Authority may by way of application on notice of motion apply to a competent court for an order cancelling or suspending the registration of a bank if in the opinion of the Authority there exist grounds, other than the grounds referred to in section 23, justifying such cancellation or suspension.

sections.heading: Restriction by Authority of activities of bank
sections.title: Restriction of bank activities by Authority
sections.summary: The Authority may restrict a bank's activities if it identifies grounds for cancellation or suspension under section 25(4). This section allows for temporary operational limitations without full cancellation, providing a mechanism for addressing compliance issues while maintaining regulatory oversight.
sections.dtia_summary: This section enables the Authority to restrict bank activities, which can impact data transfer capabilities and compliance with regulatory requirements for financial institutions.

requirements.text: The Authority may restrict the activities of a bank if it identifies grounds for cancellation or suspension under section 25(4)

sections.source_quote: The Authority may, in lieu of an application under section 25(1), by written notice to a bank in respect of which, in the opinion of the Authority, any of the circumstances mentioned in paragraphs (a) to (e), inclusive, of section 25(4) is present, restrict the activities of the institution concerned as a bank in such respects and on such conditions as the Authority may specify in the notice.

sections.heading: Cancellation of registration at request of bank
sections.title: Cancellation of bank registration via shareholder resolution
sections.summary: The Authority must cancel a bank's registration if the institution submits a special resolution approved by shareholders holding at least 75% of voting rights. This resolution authorizes cancellation and must be submitted to the Authority for processing. The Authority has no discretion in this process once the resolution is submitted.

requirements.text: The Authority shall cancel the registration of a bank upon submission to him or her by the institution concerned of a special resolution approved by the shareholders holding no less than 75 per cent of the voting rights entitled to vote on the decision authorising such cancellation.

sections.source_quote: 27. Cancellation of registration at request of bank The Authority shall cancel the registration of a bank upon submission to him or her by the institution concerned of a special resolution approved by the shareholders holding no less than 75 per cent of the voting rights entitled to vote on the decision authorising such cancellation.

sections.heading: Cancellation of registration upon winding-up
sections.title: Cancellation of bank registration upon company winding-up
sections.summary: When a bank's affairs are completely wound up under the Companies Act, the High Court's Master must transmit a certificate of winding-up to the Authority. The Authority then cancels the bank's registration upon receiving this certificate. This process ensures timely deregistration after a bank ceases operations.

requirements.text: When the affairs of a bank have been completely wound up as contemplated in section 82(1) of the Companies Act, the responsible Master of the High Court shall transmit to the Authority a copy of the certificate referred to in that section, and the Authority shall upon receipt of such copy cancel the registration of the bank concerned.

sections.source_quote: 28. Cancellation of registration upon winding-up When the affairs of a bank have been completely wound up as contemplated in section 82(1) of the Companies Act, the responsible Master of the High Court shall transmit to the Authority a copy of the certificate referred to in that section, and the Authority shall upon receipt of such copy cancel the registration of the bank concerned.

sections.heading: Withdrawal of suspension of restriction
sections.title: Withdrawal of suspension or restriction on bank registration
sections.summary: The Authority may lift a suspension or restriction on a bank's registration if the bank submits a written application and proves compliance with all conditions set by the Authority for withdrawal. This includes both suspension under section 23 and restrictions under section 26. The Authority must be satisfied with compliance before lifting the restriction.

requirements.text: The Authority may on the written application of a bank of which the registration was suspended under section 23 or the activities were restricted under section 26, by written notice to the institution concerned withdraw such suspension or restriction, as the case may be, provided the Authority is satisfied that the institution has complied with all requirements for such withdrawal imposed by the Authority in the conditions of suspension or restriction.

sections.source_quote: 29. Withdrawal of suspension of restriction (1) The Authority may on the written application of a bank of which- (a) the registration was suspended under section 23; or (b) the activities were restricted under section 26, by written notice to the institution concerned withdraw such suspension or restriction, as the case may be, provided the Authority is satisfied that the institution has complied with all requirements for such withdrawal imposed by the Authority in the conditions of suspension or restriction.

sections.heading: Publication of information relating to banks, controlling companies, eligible institutions and representative offices of
sections.title: Public publication and record-keeping of bank information
sections.summary: The Authority must publish in the Gazette and maintain records of bank registrations, cancellations, suspensions, restrictions, name changes, mergers, asset transfers exceeding 25%, and approvals for subsidiaries, branches, and foreign operations. This includes detailed records of all approvals granted under the Act for international banking activities.

requirements.text: The Authority shall publish a notice in the Gazette and shall keep a record of registration of an institution as a bank or a controlling company.
The Authority shall keep a record of cancellation or suspension of the registration of a bank or controlling company.
The Authority shall keep a record of withdrawal of the authorisation to conduct the business of a bank by means of a branch.
The Authority shall keep a record of restriction on the activities of a bank, controlling company or branch.
The Authority shall keep a record of change of name of a bank, controlling company, branch or representative office of a foreign institution.

The Authority shall keep a record of permission granted in respect of the amalgamation, merger or arrangement referred to in Chapter 5 of the Companies Act, where a bank is the principal party to such amalgamation, merger or arrangement.
The Authority shall keep a record of permission granted to an arrangement for the transfer of more than 25 per cent of the assets, liabilities or assets and liabilities of a bank to another person.
The Authority shall keep a record of approval granted in respect of an eligible institution, which is effected or which takes place under this Act.
The Authority shall keep a record of every approval granted to a bank or a controlling company to establish or acquire a subsidiary within or outside the Republic.
The Authority shall keep a record of every approval granted to a bank to establish or acquire a branch of a bank.

The Authority shall keep a record of every approval granted to a bank or a controlling company to acquire an interest in any undertaking having its registered office or principal place of business outside the Republic.
The Authority shall keep a record of every approval granted to a bank or a controlling company to create or acquire a trust outside the Republic of which the bank is a major beneficiary.

The Authority shall keep a record of every approval granted to a bank or a controlling company to establish or acquire any financial or other business undertaking under its direct or indirect control.
The Authority shall keep a record of every approval granted to a bank to establish or acquire a representative office outside the Republic.

sections.source_quote: 30. Publication of information relating to banks, controlling companies, eligible institutions and representative offices of foreign institutions and the keeping of records by the Authority (1) The Authority shall publish a notice in the Gazette and shall keep a record of- (a) (i) registration of an institution as a bank or a controlling company; (ii) authorisation granted to conduct the business of a bank by means of a branch; or (iii) consent granted for the establishment of a representative office by a foreign institution; (b) (i) cancellation or suspension of the registration of a bank or controlling company; (ii) withdrawal of the authorisation to conduct the business of a bank by means of a branch; or (iii) withdrawal of consent to conduct the business of a representative office by a foreign institution or the closure of such representative office; (c) restriction on the activities of a bank, controlling company or branch; (d) change of name of a bank, controlling company, branch or representative office of a foreign institution; (e) permission granted in respect of the amalgamation, merger or arrangement referred to in Chapter 5 of the Companies Act, where a bank is the principal party to such amalgamation, merger or arrangement; (f) permission granted to an arrangement for the transfer of more than 25 per cent of the assets, liabilities or assets and liabilities of a bank to another person; or (g) approval granted in respect of an eligible institution, which is effected or which takes place under this Act.

sections.heading: Date on which registration lapses
sections.title: Date when bank registration lapses
sections.summary: Bank registration lapses after 30 days from the cancellation notice if the bank does not appeal to the board of review within that period. If the bank appeals and the board confirms cancellation, the lapse date is when the bank is notified of confirmation. For court-ordered cancellations, the lapse date is when the court order takes effect. For cancellations under sections 27 or 28, the lapse date is determined by the Authority.

requirements.text: An institution registered as a bank shall cease to be registered as such in the case of a registration cancelled by the Authority under section 23, upon expiry of 30 days after the date of the notice referred to in subsection (1), (2) or (3) of that section or, if an application for the review of such a decision to cancel was lodged with the board of review in terms of section 9 before the expiry of the said 30 days and the board of review has confirmed such cancellation, upon the date on which the institution concerned is notified of such confirmation.

sections.source_quote: 31. Date on which registration lapses An institution registered as a bank shall cease to be registered as such- (b) in the case of a registration cancelled by the Authority under section 23, upon expiry of 30 days after the date of the notice referred to in subsection (1), (2) or (3) of that section or, if an application for the review of such a decision to cancel was lodged with the board of review in terms of section 9 before the expiry of the said 30 days and the board of review has confirmed such cancellation, upon the date on which the institution concerned is notified of such confirmation;

sections.heading: Repayment of deposits upon lapse of registration
sections.title: Repayment of deposits upon bank registration lapse
sections.summary: If a bank ceases registration, the Authority may order the bank to repay all deposits to customers within a specified period, including interest and other amounts owed. The bank must also change its name and memorandum of incorporation within the required timeframe. Failure to comply results in criminal liability and insolvency implications under the Companies Act.

requirements.text: Whenever an institution which is registered as a bank ceases to be registered as such, the Authority may in writing order that institution to repay, in accordance with such directions and within such period as may be specified in the order, all money due by it to members of the public in respect of deposits accepted by it while registered as a bank, including any interest or any other amounts owing by it in respect of such money.

An institution which by virtue of the provisions of subsection (1) repays a deposit before the due date agreed for the repayment thereof, shall not be bound to pay any interest or any other amounts which would have been payable in respect of such deposit for the period from the date of such repayment up to such due date.

An institution which fails to comply with an order under subsection (1) shall be guilty of an offence; and for the purposes of sections 344 and 345 of the Companies Act be deemed not to be able to pay its debts.

sections.source_quote: 32. Repayment of deposits upon lapse of registration (1) Whenever an institution which is registered as a bank ceases to be registered as such, the Authority may in writing order that institution- (a) to repay, in accordance with such directions and within such period as may be specified in the order, all money due by it to members of the public in respect of deposits accepted by it while registered as a bank, including any interest or any other amounts owing by it in respect of such money; and (b) to change its name and its memorandum of incorporation within the period and in the manner required by the Authority.

sections.heading: Reregistration in terms of this Act
sections.title: Reregistration of deposit-taking institutions as banks
sections.summary: This section mandates that deposit-taking institutions provisionally or finally registered under the Banks Act prior to the 1993 Amendment Act must be reregistered as banks by the Authority within a practicable timeframe. The reregistration process requires the Authority to issue a certificate of provisional or final registration as a bank. The reregistration period aligns with the unexpired portion of the institution's previous provisional registration term. Previous deposit-taking registration automatically lapses upon reregistration with no fees applicable.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes the transition mechanism for institutions moving from deposit-taking to banking registration, which impacts data transfer contexts through the regulatory framework governing financial institutions' registration status.

requirements.text: Provisionally registered deposit-taking institutions must be reregistered as banks by the Authority within practicable timeframe
Finally registered deposit-taking institutions must be reregistered as banks by the Authority within practicable timeframe
Authority issues certificate of provisional or final registration as bank upon reregistration
Reregistration period aligns with unexpired portion of previous provisional registration term
Previous deposit-taking registration lapses upon reregistration with cancellation of certificates
No fees payable for reregistration

sections.source_quote: 33. Reregistration in terms of this Act (1) Every institution which on the date immediately preceding the date of commencement of the Deposit-taking Institutions Amendment Act, 1993... shall, in accordance with and subject to the provisions of subsections (2) and (3)...

sections.heading: Representative offices of foreign institutions
sections.title: Foreign institutions' representative offices in South Africa
sections.summary: This section prohibits foreign institutions from establishing representative offices in South Africa without written consent from the Authority. The consent requires a detailed application specifying the foreign institution's name, country of establishment, proposed chief representative officer, and representative office address. The Authority may grant consent unconditionally or with conditions, including verification of the foreign institution's business authorization, compliance with Basel Committee guidelines, and public interest considerations. Representative offices must notify the Authority of any changes and cannot conduct banking business.
sections.dtia_summary: This section governs foreign entities establishing representative offices in South Africa, which impacts data transfer contexts through the regulatory framework governing foreign entities' data handling activities.

requirements.text: Foreign institutions may not establish representative offices in South Africa without written consent from the Authority
Application for consent must specify foreign institution name, country of establishment, proposed chief representative officer, and representative office address
Application must include certificate from competent authority in foreign country authorizing business similar to banking
Authority may grant consent unconditionally or with conditions
Authority must verify foreign institution lawfully conducts banking-like business in foreign country
Authority must verify foreign country's supervisory authority accepts Basel Committee guidelines
Authority must verify foreign country's supervisory authority is not legally precluded from fulfilling obligations
Authority must verify foreign country's supervisory authority will provide financial soundness information continuously
Authority must verify establishment of representative office will not be detrimental to public interest
Authority issues certificate of authorization for representative office establishment upon approval
Foreign institution must notify Authority of name changes, chief representative officer changes, address changes, or office closures
Representative offices may not conduct banking business in South Africa
Representative offices must provide prescribed information to Authority as required
Representative offices must conduct business subject to prescribed conditions

sections.source_quote: 34. Representative offices of foreign institutions (1) An institution which has been established in a country other than the Republic and which lawfully conducts in such other country a business similar to the business of a bank... may not establish representative offices in South Africa without written consent from the Authority...

sections.heading: Annual licence
Permission for acquisition of shares in bank or controlling company
Registration of shares in name of nominees
sections.title: Share registration in name of nominees
sections.summary: This section prohibits banks and controlling companies from registering shares in the name of persons other than beneficial shareholders without written approval from the Authority. Exceptions include collective investment schemes, estate representatives, regulated persons for up to six months, and other special circumstances determined by the Minister. The Authority may approve share registration in the name of nominees for specific purposes.
sections.dtia_summary: This section governs share registration practices that impact data transfer contexts through the regulatory framework governing financial institutions' shareholder records.

requirements.text: Banks or controlling companies may not register shares in name of persons other than beneficial shareholders without Authority approval
Authority may approve share registration in name of estate representatives
Authority may approve share registration in name of regulated persons for up to six months
Authority may approve share registration in name of persons in special circumstances determined by Minister
Authority may approve share registration in name of central securities depositories

sections.source_quote: 38. Registration of shares in name of nominees (1) Notwithstanding the provisions of the Companies Act, no bank or controlling company shall without the written approval of the Authority... allot or issue any of its shares to, or register any of its shares in the name of, any person other than the intended beneficial shareholder...

sections.heading: Furnishing of information by shareholders
Absence of wrongful intent
sections.title: Absence of wrongful intent for bank contraventions
sections.summary: This section states that if a bank, its controlling company, or any director, officer, employee, or agent acts in good faith and unknowingly contravenes section 38, such actions do not constitute an offence. The provision explicitly excludes liability for unintentional breaches when acting on reasonably obtained information.

requirements.text: If a bank or a controlling company or any director, officer, employee or agent acts in good faith and on reasonably obtained information, unknowingly contravening section 38, such act does not constitute an offence.

sections.source_quote: 40. Absence of wrongful intent. If a bank or a controlling company or any director, officer, employee or agent of a bank or controlling company in good faith and on the strength of information reasonably obtained acts or fails to act and thereby unknowingly contravenes the provisions of section 38, such act or failure to act shall not constitute an offence.

sections.heading: Effects of registration of shares contrary to Act
sections.title: Prohibition on voting or dividends for shares issued contrary to Act
sections.summary: This section prohibits any person from voting on or receiving dividends for shares in a bank or controlling company that were allotted or registered in contravention of the Act. It also states that resolutions adopted by a bank or controlling company remain valid if the vote was cast in contravention of this provision but the resolution was approved by the requisite majority of valid votes.

requirements.text: No person may personally or by proxy cast a vote attached to shares in a bank or controlling company that were allotted or registered in contravention of this Act.
No person may receive dividends payable on shares in a bank or controlling company that were allotted or registered in contravention of this Act.
A resolution adopted by a bank or controlling company remains valid if it was approved by the requisite majority of valid votes despite votes cast in contravention of this section.
Dividends payable on shares in contravention of this Act accrue to the bank or controlling company concerned.

sections.source_quote: 41. Effects of registration of shares contrary to Act. (1) No person shall- (a) either personally or by proxy granted to any other person, cast a vote attached to; or (b) receive a dividend payable on, any share in a bank or controlling company allotted or issued to such person or registered in the name of such person in contravention of a provision of this Act.

sections.heading: Restriction of right to control bank
sections.title: Restrictions on controlling banks outside Republic
sections.summary: This section restricts who can exercise control over a bank in South Africa. Only banks or institutions approved by the Authority that conduct similar banking business in other countries may control a bank, provided they are public companies registered as controlling companies. It defines control through shareholding (over 50% nominal value), voting rights, or director appointment powers.

requirements.text: No person other than a bank or an approved institution may exercise control over a bank unless they are a public company registered as a controlling company.

A person exercises control over a bank if they hold shares representing more than 50% nominal value (excluding voting limitations) or have voting rights exceeding 50% or can determine the appointment of the majority of directors.
A person's appointment as a director of a bank follows from their appointment as a director of a controlling company, which counts as exercising control.

sections.source_quote: 42. Restriction of right to control bank. (1) Subject to the provisions of section 37, no person other than a bank or an institution which has been approved by the Authority and which conducts business similar to the business of a bank in a country other than the Republic may exercise control over a bank, unless such person is a public company and is registered as a controlling company in respect of such bank.

sections.heading: Application for registration as controlling company
sections.title: Application for registration as controlling company
sections.summary: This section outlines the process for applying to the Authority as a controlling company. Public companies intending to control a bank or holding companies for other banks must submit applications with required information and documents. The Authority may require additional particulars during the application process.

requirements.text: A public company intending to exercise control over a bank must apply to the Authority for registration as a controlling company.
A holding company for another public company applying to register as a bank must apply to the Authority for registration as a controlling company.
An application must be accompanied by information and documents as prescribed by the Authority.
A public company applying for registration must submit additional particulars as required by the Authority.

sections.source_quote: 43. Application for registration as controlling company. (1) Subject to section 42, a public company- (a) that intends to exercise control over any bank; or (b) which is a holding company, as defined in section 1 of the Companies Act, in respect of any other public company which has applied in terms of section 16 for registration as a bank, shall apply to the Authority on the form prescribed or registration as a controlling company in respect of that bank or proposed bank, as the case may be.

sections.heading: Granting or refusal of application for registration as controlling company
sections.title: Granting or refusal of registration as controlling company
sections.summary: This section details the Authority's process for granting or refusing applications to register as a controlling company. The Authority must ensure the applicant will not be contrary to public interest, can establish control over the bank, has a valid memorandum of incorporation, has fit and proper directors, is financially sound, and complies with the Act. The Authority may impose conditions such as proof of control or share offers to existing shareholders.

requirements.text: The Authority may grant or refuse an application for registration as a controlling company after considering all information and documents.
The Authority must be satisfied the registration will not be contrary to public interest.
The Authority must be satisfied the applicant can establish control over the bank as defined in section 42(2).
The Authority must be satisfied the applicant's memorandum of incorporation is not inconsistent with this Act.
The Authority must be satisfied all directors and executive officers are fit and proper and have sufficient knowledge and experience.
The Authority must be satisfied the applicant is financially sound.
The Authority must be satisfied no interest of the applicant is inconsistent with this Act.
The Authority must be satisfied the application complies with the Act.
The Authority may impose conditions requiring proof of control within a specified period.
The Authority may impose conditions requiring an offer to shareholders to take up shares in the applicant.

sections.source_quote: 44. Granting or refusal of application for registration as controlling company. (1) Subject to the provisions of subsection (2), the Authority may, after considering all information, documents and particulars furnished in terms of section 43 for the purposes of an application under that section, grant or refuse the relevant application or grant the application subject to such conditions as he or she may impose.

sections.heading: Cancellation by Authority of registration of controlling company
sections.title: Cancellation of controlling company registration
sections.summary: This section allows the Authority to cancel the registration of a controlling company if it fails to establish control over the bank or no longer exercises such control. The Authority must provide the controlling company with a 30-day opportunity to show cause before cancellation.

requirements.text: The Authority may cancel registration of a controlling company if it fails to establish control over the bank or no longer exercises such control.
The Authority must provide the controlling company with a 30-day opportunity to show cause before cancelling registration.

sections.source_quote: 45. Cancellation by Authority of registration of controlling company. (1) If a controlling company has failed to establish control over the bank in respect of which it is registered, or no longer exercises such control, the Authority may by notice in writing to such controlling company cancel its registration in respect of that bank.

sections.heading: Cancellation by court of registration of controlling company
sections.title: Court cancellation of controlling company registration
sections.summary: The Authority may apply to a court for cancellation of a controlling company's registration if the Authority believes there are grounds beyond those in section 45. The court may grant cancellation if the controlling company provided materially untrue information or failed to comply with the Act, or if the court considers it not in the public interest to allow the company to continue.

requirements.text: The Authority may apply to a court for cancellation of a controlling company's registration if grounds exist beyond those in section 45
The court may cancel registration if the controlling company provided materially untrue information
The court may cancel registration if the controlling company failed to comply with the Act
The court may cancel registration if it is not in the public interest to allow the company to continue

sections.source_quote: 46. Cancellation by court of registration of controlling company (1) The Authority may by way of application on notice of motion apply to a competent court for an order cancelling the registration of a controlling company if in the opinion of the Authority there exist grounds, other than the grounds referred to in section 45, justifying such cancellation.

sections.heading: Cancellation of registration at request of controlling company
sections.title: Controlling company registration cancellation by shareholders
sections.summary: The Authority must cancel a controlling company's registration when the company submits a special resolution approved by shareholders holding at least 75% of voting rights authorizing cancellation. This resolution must be submitted to the Authority for cancellation.

requirements.text: The Authority shall cancel registration upon submission of a special resolution approved by shareholders holding at least 75% of voting rights

sections.source_quote: 47. Cancellation of registration at request of controlling company The Authority shall cancel the registration of the controlling company upon submission to him or her by the controlling company of a special resolution approved by the shareholders holding no less than 75 per cent of the voting rights entitled to vote on the decision authorising such cancellation.

sections.heading: Lapse of registration of controlling company upon cancellation of registration of bank
sections.title: Lapse of controlling company registration upon bank cancellation
sections.summary: When a bank's registration is cancelled, the controlling company's registration for that bank automatically lapses simultaneously. This lapse takes effect from the date the bank ceased to be registered as such.

requirements.text: The registration of a controlling company lapses simultaneously with the cancellation of the bank's registration
The lapse takes effect from the date the bank ceased to be registered as such

sections.source_quote: 48. Lapse of registration of controlling company upon cancellation of registration of bank (1) If the registration of a bank in respect of which a controlling company is registered, is cancelled, the registration of that controlling company in respect of that bank shall be deemed to have been cancelled simultaneously.

sections.heading: Date on which registration of controlling company lapses
sections.title: Lapse date for controlling company registration
sections.summary: A controlling company's registration lapses on specific dates: 30 days after notice of cancellation under section 45, the date the court order under section 46 comes into force, or a date determined by the Authority for cancellations under section 47.

requirements.text: Registration lapses 30 days after notice of cancellation under section 45
Registration lapses on the date the court order under section 46 comes into force
Registration lapses on a date determined by the Authority for cancellations under section 47

sections.source_quote: 49. Date on which registration of controlling company lapses A controlling company shall cease to be registered as such- (a) in the case of a registration cancelled by the Authority under section 45, upon expiry of 30 days after the date of the notice referred to in subsection (1) of that section or, if an application for the review of such a decision to cancel was lodged with the board of review in terms of section 9 before the expiry of the said 30 days and the board of review has confirmed such cancellation, upon the date on which the controlling company concerned is notified of such confirmation;

sections.heading: Investments and loans and advances by controlling companies
sections.title: Investments and loans by controlling companies
sections.summary: Controlling companies must limit investments and loans to specific entities and ensure that the amount of such investments or loans does not exceed a prescribed percentage of their share capital and reserve funds, calculated on a consolidated basis.

requirements.text: Controlling companies must limit investments in non-bank entities to a prescribed percentage of share capital and reserve funds
Controlling companies must limit loans to non-bank entities to a prescribed percentage of share capital and reserve funds

sections.source_quote: 50. Investments and loans and advances by controlling companies (1) A controlling company investing money- (a) in undertakings other than banks or institutions which conduct business similar to the business of a bank in a country other than the Republic, controlling companies or companies of which the main object is the holding or development of property which is used or intended to be used mainly for the purpose of conducting the business of a bank; or (b) in fixed property which is not used or intended to be used mainly for the purpose of conducting the business of a bank, shall manage its transactions in such investments in such a way that the amount of such investments does not at any time exceed a prescribed percentage of a prescribed amount of the share capital and reserve funds of the controlling company calculated on a consolidated basis as prescribed.

sections.heading: Application of Companies Act to banks and controlling companies
sections.title: Application of Companies Act to banks and controlling companies
sections.summary: Banks and controlling companies remain subject to the Companies Act except for specific exclusions: conversion provisions do not apply, business rescue provisions do not apply to banks, and the Minister may delegate authority to the Authority for certain provisions.

requirements.text: Banks and controlling companies remain subject to the Companies Act except for specific exclusions
Conversion provisions of the Companies Act do not apply to banks or controlling companies
Business rescue provisions of the Companies Act do not apply to banks
The Minister may delegate authority to the Authority for certain provisions of the Companies Act

sections.source_quote: 51. Application of Companies Act to banks and controlling companies (1) A company registered as a bank or as a controlling company shall continue to be a company in terms of the Companies Act, and the provisions of that Act shall, subject to the provisions of subsection (2), continue to apply to any such company to the extent to which they are not inconsistent with any provision of this Act: Provided that- (a) the provisions of the Companies Act governing the conversion of public companies into other forms of companies shall not apply to any such company; (b) the provisions of sections 128 to 154 of the Companies Act relating to business rescue shall not apply to a bank;

sections.heading: Subsidiaries, branch offices, other interests and representative offices of banks and controlling companies
sections.title: Bank subsidiaries and foreign offices require prior approval for establishment
sections.summary: Banks must obtain prior written approval from the Authority for establishing or acquiring subsidiaries, branch offices, or representative offices outside South Africa. The Authority may set conditions for such actions, including limits on investments in joint ventures exceeding 5% of capital. Banks must notify the Authority of changes to representative offices and provide specific details for foreign representative offices.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires explicit approval for cross-border establishment of subsidiaries and offices, with specific thresholds for joint ventures. This impacts data transfer DTIAs by mandating regulatory oversight of foreign entities handling bank data.

requirements.text: Bank must obtain prior written approval from Authority for establishing or acquiring subsidiaries outside Republic
Bank must obtain prior written approval from Authority for acquiring branch offices outside Republic
Bank must obtain prior written approval from Authority for acquiring interests in foreign undertakings
Bank must obtain prior written approval from Authority for creating or acquiring trusts where it is a major beneficiary outside Republic
Bank must obtain prior written approval from Authority for establishing or acquiring foreign divisions through agreements
Bank must notify Authority of representative office address changes within 24 hours
Bank must notify Authority of representative office chief representative officer substitutions within 24 hours
Bank must notify Authority of representative office closures within 24 hours
Authority may issue directives for certain acquisitions without prior approval

sections.heading: Disclosure by banks and controlling companies of interest in subsidiaries, trusts and other undertakings
sections.title: Banks must disclose foreign subsidiaries and trusts
sections.summary: Banks and controlling companies must disclose their shareholdings and interests in foreign subsidiaries, joint ventures, and trusts to the Authority. This disclosure is required on a specified form at intervals prescribed by the Authority, covering entities referenced in section 52(1).
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates disclosure of foreign entities holding bank data, critical for DTIAs to assess data flows through subsidiaries and trusts.

requirements.text: Banks must disclose shareholdings in foreign subsidiaries
Banks must disclose interests in joint ventures
